sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Info action to introduce basic ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance into public discussion and encourage collaboration on future refinements, process amendments, and constitutional amendments.
Stated amount: not stated in document
Deliverables: Sketch basic ideas for reforming treasury governance, Outline guardrails for strategic direction, Introduce treasury-governance reform ideas into public discussion

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state because of a funding impasse and weak direct support in the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says treasury withdrawal actions have created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, ill will, and ecosystem-harming strife.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues that treasury governance should provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and the constitution.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues that the Net Change Limit should not be treated as the overall treasury budget and is only a vague initial approximation for budgeting.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal calls for a dedicated investment budget that includes strategic direction and a roadmap.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state because of a funding impasse and weak direct support in the existing governance system.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says treasury withdrawal actions have created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, ill will, and ecosystem-harming strife.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal argues that treasury governance should provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state because of a funding impasse and weak direct support in the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
